title: Parallelizing Container Builds
body: I'm working on a project for a distributed systems class in which I parallelize container builds in order to speed up build time, particularly on distributed systems.  I was initially building the functionality into buildah, but it seems like the functionality could just as well go into openshift/imagebuilder.  Do you feel that either project would find this feature useful in production?  Also, given that I'm not too familiar yet with openshift/imagebuilder, should I be adding functionality there instead?  It looks like the code I would be adding exists somewhere around the spot where the buildah executes the build, which it calls into imagebuilder for.

comments.body: Ryan it is probably best if you opened a review here, and then allow us to figure out if it should go somewhere else.
@rhatdan For the most part, yes.  There's some bugs that still need to be worked out
Any chance you can open a PR so we could examine it?

I'd be happy to, but I should probably clean up the code a bit first.  A few days before the project was due I resorted to writing bad code in order to get it done, including modifying some of the code in the vendored packages.

Also, the parallelization requires the use of one master node (buildah) and any number of workers, for which I wrote the code in a new repository.  Do you want me to try to integrate the worker code into buildah or just include a link to the worker code in my PR (in which case, if you would like, that repo can be transferred over to projectatomic).
I don't mind vendoring it in. But I think it would be worth while for a few of us to look at it, including @nalind to see what he thinks.
Sounds good, I'll clean up some of the code and then submit a PR
I do not, the bug I mentioned earlier prevented me from getting accurate results.  However, once I have that worked out I can share the results with you
@14rcole welcome back from vacation and a little reminder nudge!
CLosing as I believe this proved to be unsuccessful
